input.background: Assume there are 10 identical units to place on test at the same time t = 0 where each unit has an exponential life distribution with a mean time to failure MTTF μ = 5 h and that the units operate independently.
input.question: Determine the expected and the variance of number of failures during the first hour, i.e. interval [0, 1).

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: Let N1 be the number of failures during the first hour, in the time interval [0, 1). The probability of failure of a unit in the time interval [0,1) is q1=1-e^(-1/5)=0.1813. The distribution of N1 that the probability of exactly i failures in [0,1) is P(N1=i)=C(10,i)(0.1813)^i(0.8187)^(10-i). The expected number of failures (N1) during the first hour is E(N1)=n*q1=10*(0.1813)=1.813. The variance of N1 is V(N1)=n*q1*(1-q1)=10*(0.1813)*(0.8187)=1.4843.

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
